title: Matangaputta
author: Thanissaro Bhikkhu
contentHtml: It's too cold, too hot, too late in the evening — people who say this, shirking their work: the moment passes them by. Whoever regards cold & heat as no more than grass, doing his manly duties, won't fall away from ease. With my chest I push through wild grasses — spear-grass, ribbon-grass, rushes — cultivating a seclusion heart.
summary: It's too hot, it's too cold — what's your excuse?
